claim: Breed explains just 9% of behavioral variation in individual dogs, while most behavioral traits are heritable (h²>25%), meaning the remaining variance is driven by individual genetics and environment not captured by breed ancestry.
stance_target: breed as meaningful predictor of individual dog behavioral phenotype
scope_conditions: Darwin's Ark; 18,385 surveyed dogs (49% purebred, 85% US), 2,155 genome-sequenced; Science 2022; GWAS + factor analysis of 8 behavioral factors
quoted_support: Breed explains just 9% of behavioral variation in individuals. Genome-wide association analyses identify 11 loci that are significantly associated with behavior, and characteristic breed behaviors exhibit genetic complexity. Behavioral loci are not unusually differentiated in breeds.

claim: Agonistic threshold — how easily a dog is provoked by frightening or uncomfortable stimuli — is the LEAST breed-predictable behavioral trait; breed is 'almost uninformative' for this dimension, which is the key dimension for xenophobic lost-dog classification.
stance_target: breed as predictor of fear/reactivity in lost dog behavioral classification
scope_conditions: Morrill et al. 2022; Darwin's Ark cohort; GWAS + mixed-breed ancestry analysis; h²SNP analysis
quoted_support: For less heritable, less breed-differentiated traits, like agonistic threshold (how easily a dog is provoked by frightening or uncomfortable stimuli), breed is almost uninformative.

claim: Biddability (responsiveness to commands) is the most breed-differentiated behavioral trait; border collie ancestry has a small but genetically confirmed effect on biddability scores in mixed-breed dogs.
stance_target: limited, conditional validity of breed priors for trainability/direction-following
scope_conditions: Morrill et al. 2022; Darwin's Ark; mixed-breed ancestry admixture analysis; border collie ancestry vs. biddability factor scores
quoted_support: For more heritable, more breed-differentiated traits, like biddability (responsiveness to direction and commands), knowing breed ancestry can make behavioral predictions somewhat more accurate. For some traits, like biddability and border collie ancestry, we confirm a genetic effect of breed that aligns with survey responses.

claim: Modern dog breeds are only ~160 years old (~50-80 generations) — a blink in evolutionary history compared with dog origins >10,000 years ago — meaning breed-defining genetic variants had minimal time to accumulate in behavioral genes.
stance_target: explanation for why breed explains little behavioral variance
scope_conditions: Morrill et al. 2022; genomic analysis of 332 breed-exclusive fixed SNPs out of 16.7 million total (0.002%)
quoted_support: Modern dog breeds are less than 160 years old (~50 to 80 generations), a blink in evolutionary history compared with the origin of dogs more than 10,000 years ago. Breeds are not distinguished by a small number of 'breed-defining' variants.

claim: Physical traits are exceptionally heritable (5/8 exceed 85% h²SNP) and strongly differentiated by breed, while behavioral traits are less heritable and far less breed-differentiated — the genetic architecture is fundamentally different.
stance_target: equivalence of physical and behavioral breed predictability
scope_conditions: Morrill et al. 2022; h²SNP calculated from 1,967 dogs with genetic + survey data; 8,518,951 autosomal SNPs
quoted_support: Physical traits are exceptionally heritable, with five out of eight exceeding 85% heritability. Retrieving is the most heritable behavioral trait [52.5 ± 9.2% (±SE)], and human sociability is the most heritable factor (factor 1, 67.3 ± 13.0%). Regions associated with aesthetic traits are unusually differentiated in breeds, consistent with a history of selection, but those associated with behavior are not.

claim: 66% of mixed-breed dogs carry >5% ancestry from 4+ breeds; only 17% are mixes of just two breeds — making breed-based behavioral prediction for shelter intake populations practically impossible.
stance_target: breed-based behavioral prediction for rescue/shelter dog populations
scope_conditions: Morrill et al. 2022; genetic ancestry inference pipeline; 1,715 Darwin's Ark dogs sequenced; US shelter population context
quoted_support: Mutts are rarely (17%) mixes of just two breeds. Most (66%) carry >5% ancestry from four or more breeds.
